untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of: (1) probe depth measurement in ATR units with parametric thresholds; (2) bar-count reversal confirmation logic; (3) volume-weighted conviction assessment; (4) reentry depth as percentage of probe magnitude; (5) support/resistance level identification and 'midpoint' calculation. These require multi-bar lookback logic, parametric ATR comparisons, and synthetic level calculations not expressible in the allowed 3-field condition format. The signal is fundamentally a pattern recognition rule requiring sequential bar analysis and relative magnitude calculations beyond point-in-time indicator values.
translation_notes: This signal describes a 'spring' and 'upthrust' reversal pattern detection requiring: probe extreme identification, volume analysis, confirmation bar sequencing, and depth/reentry percentage calculations. Attempted approximation uses pivot-based support/resistance (s1/r1) and true_range < atr_14 as weak proxy for 'low conviction probe', but this does not capture the core logic of probe depth limits, bar-count reversals, volume below average, or confirmation sequence requirements. A proper implementation requires custom pattern-matching logic outside this schema.
